mnemonics: Authentication is the process of verifying identity
Passwords should be unique and complex
Dynamic schemes adapt to changing security needs
keyPoints: Pseudorandom number generators (PRNGs) produce predictable numbers
Cryptographically secure pseudorandom number generators (CSPRNGs) produce unpredictable numbers
Entity authentication ensures the identity of communicating parties
Password hashing and salting ensure password security
Time-based one-time passwords (TOTP) and HMAC-based one-time passwords (HOTP) provide dynamic password schemes
Zero-knowledge proofs allow parties to prove statements without revealing information
